A '''mission''' took place on the [[Planet/Legends|planet]] of [[Dorlo]] during the [[Galactic Civil War/Legends|Galactic Civil War]].

==Background==
[[File:Qurahn.png|thumb|right|150px|Jedi Master Rahn]]
The [[Jedi/Legends|Jedi]] Master [[Qu Rahn/Legends|Qu Rahn]] had learned of the location of a mythical location called the [[Valley of the Jedi]], and had decided to assemble a team to investigate the valley, located on the [[Planet/Legends|planet]] [[Ruusan/Legends|Ruusan]]. By [[5 ABY/Legends|5 ABY]], after the [[Battle of Endor/Legends|defeat]] of the [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Galactic Empire]], Rahn assembled a team, consisted of the young [[Human/Legends|human]] [[Pilot/Legends|pilot]] [[Duno Dree]], [[Alliance to Restore the Republic/Legends|Rebel Alliance]] weapons expert [[Cee Norley]] and a [[Klatooinian/Legends|Klatooinian]] [[Tech specialist|technologist]] [[Rolanda Gron]]. Rahn's team needed one more member: [[Nij Por Ral]], an expert at ancient linguistics located on the planet [[Dorlo]], where he was working on translating writing carved into ancient ruins uncovered by [[Mining/Legends|miners]] working for the [[SoroSuub Corporation/Legends|SoroSuub Corporation]]. Rahn wanted to recruit Por Ral so that he could help decipher any glyphs that were found in the Valley of the Jedi. During the mission, Dree developed an emotional attachment to Norley, but was afraid to admit his feelings to her.<ref name="RA">''[[Dark Forces: Rebel Agent]]''</ref>

Dree took the existing team on [[Duno Dree's transport|his transport]] to Dorlo, landed, and camouflaged the ship. Rahn and the team managed to locate Por Ral and convinced the reluctant linguist to join them on their quest. What seemed to be a lark mission, however, quickly turned into a life or death struggle. They had been pursued by agents of the [[Imperial Remnant]], led by the [[Dark Jedi/Legends|Dark Jedi]] [[Jerec (Inquisitor)|Jerec]] from [[Vengeance (Vengeance-class)|''Vengeance'']]; Jerec was Rahn's adversary and rival in finding the lost valley and ordered his forces to capture Rahn.<ref name="RA" />

==Pursuit==
During their way back, the Rebels's [[Skimmer/Legends|skimmer]] received blaster fire, Rahn was grazed by a [[Laser/Legends|laser]] and was smeared in allied [[Blood/Legends|blood]]. The rebel team began their return journey to the [[Corvette/Legends|corvette]]. Rahn offered Dree and the others words of encouragement<ref name="RA" /> before abandoning the skimmer to continue their trek on foot; Rahn knew that the Imperials would track them via the vessel's heat signature and it would all be over.<ref name="AD" />

The Rebels were pursued by two Imperial skimmers, one had twenty stormtroopers<ref name="AD" /> while the other was led by Jerec's lieutenant [[Sariss]] and [[Maw (Dark Jedi)|Maw]], and with them was the young [[Yun]] in what was his first mission; these were three of the [[Seven Dark Jedi]] trained under Jerec.<ref name="RA" /> At first the skimmer instruments showed no sign of the Rebels, not even when Yun cross-checked instruments and scanners of both vessels.<ref name="AD" /> However the [[Boltrunian/Legends|Boltrunian]] Maw actually had the talent of [[Force sense/Legends|smelling the fear]] of his prey through [[The Force/Legends|the Force]].<ref name="AD" /><ref name="RA" /> Jerec called Sariss, momentarily driving her off-balance, expressing curiosity and impatience for the pursue as the ''Vengeance'' had no readings either; Sariss assured him that they were on their tail thanks to Maw, whose powers Jerec trusted.<ref name="AD" />

The Rebel group eventually came to a halt among the trees for cover, as even Norley, the only real [[Soldier/Legends|soldier]] among them, needed to catch her breath; she was always carrying two [[Missile launcher/Legends|missile launchers]]. Dree complained that they should have continued to use the skimmer, and that he could have flown them to the ship and back several times already. Rahn commended Dree for his self-esteem, and explained his reasons for abandoning the vessel. Norley further complained that the others weren't up the task, despite their objections. With time running out, Rahn devised a plan that would have Norley stay behind and buy the others time to escape.<ref name="AD">[[Dark Forces: Rebel Agent (audio drama)|''Dark Forces: Rebel Agent'' audio drama]]</ref>

With Maw giving orders to an Imperial pilot,<ref name="RA" /> the two skimmers followed the tail of the Rebels to the south-east.<ref name="AD" /> At some point Rahn's plan was triggered, as Norley, armed her launchers, ambushed them and managed to destroy the skimmer carrying the stormtroopers. As the other was closing, Norley was ready to shoot at it but couldn't make it, as Sariss [[Telekinesis/Legends#Force Choke|choked]] her and shot her in time<ref name="RA" /> while Yun in his rage continued blindly to shoot at her even after her death. While Yun believed that the Rebel wanted the Imperials dead, Sariss knew that war was not in Rahn's interests;<ref name="AD" /> Norley had just sacrificed herself only to buy her companions some time, and this meant to Sariss that the others were trying to make it to a ship and leave the planet.<ref name="RA" /> She ordered the pilot to resume their original course.<ref name="AD" />

==The battle==
[[File:Battle_of_Dorlo.png|thumb|left|280px|The [[Vengeance (Vengeance-class)|''Vengeance'']] over [[Dorlo]].]]
Attempting to escape the planet, Dree's [[CR90 corvette/Legends|Corvette]] skipped some initiation procedures in haste; once off, he took a turn and attacked the pursuing skimmer. Although he failed to hit them, it veered off the slipstream and hit a stone wall. The three Dark Jedi and their pilot recovered, but as the pilot complained about a lump on his head, he was decapitated by a [[Lightsaber/Legends|lightsaber]].<ref name="RA" /><ref name="AD" />

The corvette reached space, pursued by four [[TIE/ln space superiority starfighter/Legends|TIE fighters]], which actually led her into a trap; despite Rahn's warnings, Dree couldn't see Jerec's flagship, [[Vengeance (Vengeance-class)|''Vengeance'']], and flew right towards its [[Tractor beam/Legends|tractor beams]].<ref name="AD" /> In vain Dree struggled to dampen the runaway power plants.<ref name="RA" />

The Rebel corvette was boarded by an Imperial [[Assault shuttle/Legends|assault shuttle]] and a task force of [[Commando/Legends|Commandos]] led by [[Boc Aseca]]. After they had filled the areas with [[Sleeping gas]], the occupants fell on their way to the storage lockers with the [[Environment suit/Legends|space suits]].<ref name="AD" /> They were carried out on stretchers and taken to Jerec for interrogation. Their transport was used for target practice by the ''Vengeance''{{'s}} crew and blown up.

==Aftermath==
[[File:Rahn_hit.png|right|thumb|250px|Rahn paralyzed by a [[Force Destruction|Force attack]] from Jerec.]]
The prisoners were brought to ''Vengeance''{{'s}} bridge, where Jerec and his Dark Jedi awaited them. Jerec questioned the party about the location of the mystical [[Valley of the Jedi]].

With each refusal to cooperate, one member of Rahn's party was executed by Jerec's Dark Jedi underlings. In the end, the Jedi Master succumbed to Jerec's powers, and a hint of the pathway leading to the Valley was revealed.

In a final attempt to stop the dark-siders, Rahn stole one of their lightsabers and fought valiantly, until Jerec subdued and killed him.

==Behind the scenes==
It is suggested in the novelization that Rahn's team included at least one more Rebel: in his first appearance, his white robe is already "red with Rebel blood" but without any of his team, or himself, mentioned to be wounded; this means that an unnamed companion was killed in a skirmish before or during the pursuit.

In the novelization, after the darksiders's skimmer is thrown down by Dree's ship, it is Maw who decapitates the helmsman for his mistake; in the dramatization it is Sariss who does this

==Appearances==
*''[[Dark Forces: Rebel Agent]]''
*''[[Star Wars: Jedi Knight: Dark Forces II]]'' {{1st}}
